mentions.note: Caller Buddy owned at $250, now $156; concerns about Red Canary acquisition, stock-based comp, rule of 62 vs. rule of 40 confusion; Cramer advises hold, lighten on bounce if overconcentrated in cyber.
Enterprise software/security company vulnerable to AI disruption; part of cohort losing market share to AI-native competitors.
Down 38% Q1 on cybersecurity displacement fears wrongly applied; CrowdStrike/Palo Alto prove security actually gaining from AI—not vulnerable; prefer CrowdStrike/Palo Alto over Zscaler though.
Once red-hot software security play disappointed on latest earnings; market pivot toward hardware over software leaving software valuations vulnerable.
